name: BMA
description: External government entity responsible for final disposal and compression bins

name: Units and offices
description: Internal departments that generate waste and label it for ownership

name: Finance Division
description: Processes data to manage money transfers and payments to units

name: N15
description: Receiver of cleaned fuel waste (RDF)

name: Mirror Foundation
description: Charitable organization using donated e-waste for rural computer repairs

name: Housekeepers / Maids
description: Staff responsible for collecting weighing and moving waste

name: Waste holding cage
description: The designated storage area for waste before pickup

name: Garden team
description: Staff responsible for external waste collection across campus

name: Waste sorting plant
description: The facility where waste is sorted before being sent to BMA

name: Students
description: Primary waste generators and occasional volunteers for sorting

name: Working committee
description: Internal oversight group that monitors waste levels and issues warnings

name: Gray bins
description: Containers specifically for construction waste like iron and cement

name: Yellow BMA bins
description: Compression bins used for unsortable waste

name: Infectious waste bins
description: Bins for COVID-related waste like masks and ATK kits

name: E-waste
description: Electronic waste often exchanged for student activity hours

name: Construction waste
description: Bulky waste including wood and plywood from events

name: Recyclable waste
description: Waste collected in specific monthly rounds

name: Trash weight data
description: Information recorded in notebooks regarding waste volume

name: Revenue
description: Financial proceeds shared 80% to units and 20% to the university

name: Pests (Rats/Ants)
description: Organisms attracted to food waste left in offices

name: Waste residue
description: Leftover waste that overflows when collection rounds are missed
